abstract: Estimate achievable X-gate speeds and fidelities for a Yb-171 nuclear qubit using Raman transfer via a long-lived inner-shell J=2 intermediate (with 1 W/cm^2 available), limited by hyperfine splitting versus intermediate lifetime, and report gate-time vs target-infidelity tradeoffs (10^-2-10^-5).
artifacts_summary: Resumed writing_presentation completed with a compile-ready conference-template manuscript, synchronized bibliography and equation-usage exports, and refreshed execution evidence for the manual resume iteration.
constraints: Available optical intensity is fixed at 1 W/cm^2.
Qubit encoding fixed to Yb-171 3P0 with F=1/2 two-level manifold.
Gate mechanism constrained to Raman transfer through inner-shell J=2 intermediate.
Tradeoff table must cover target infidelities 10^-2, 10^-3, 10^-4, and 10^-5.
evaluation_plan: Produce a quantitative table of estimated gate times/speeds achievable at fixed 1 W/cm^2 for fidelity targets 1-10^-2, 1-10^-3, 1-10^-4, and 1-10^-5; compare regimes and identify the fastest operating point satisfying infidelity <=10^-3.
full_description: The user requests a feasibility estimate for Raman-driven X gates on a Yb-171 nuclear qubit encoded in the 3P0 F=1/2 manifold, using an inner-shell-excited J=2 intermediate state reached through a weak electric-quadrupole pathway. The core tradeoff is gate speed versus achievable infidelity under finite laser intensity (1 W/cm^2), with error behavior framed by hyperfine splitting relative to intermediate-state lifetime. Resource context supports that the Yb inner-shell 4f^13 5d6s^2 (J=2) transition is experimentally observed and weakly allowed with resolved hyperfine/Zeeman structure, while earlier theory identifies this J=2 manifold as metastable and relevant for precision-clock operation. The requested output is a quantitative gate-time versus target-infidelity summary over 10^-2, 10^-3, 10^-4, and 10^-5, prioritizing fastest gates at <=10^-3 infidelity.
goals: Estimate achievable X-gate speed for Yb-171 3P0 nuclear qubit using Raman transfer via inner-shell J=2 intermediate.
Estimate achievable gate fidelity under 1 W/cm^2 available intensity.
Quantify gate-time versus target-infidelity tradeoffs for targets 10^-2, 10^-3, 10^-4, and 10^-5.
Prioritize fastest gate regime meeting <=10^-3 infidelity.

constraints: Available optical intensity is fixed at 1 W/cm^2.
Qubit encoding fixed to Yb-171 3P0 with F=1/2 two-level manifold.
Gate mechanism constrained to Raman transfer through inner-shell J=2 intermediate.
Tradeoff table must cover target infidelities 10^-2, 10^-3, 10^-4, and 10^-5.
original_problem.evaluation_plan: Produce a quantitative table of estimated gate times/speeds achievable at fixed 1 W/cm^2 for fidelity targets 1-10^-2, 1-10^-3, 1-10^-4, and 1-10^-5; compare regimes and identify the fastest operating point satisfying infidelity <=10^-3.

The core tradeoff is gate speed versus achievable infidelity under finite laser intensity (1 W/cm^2), with error behavior framed by hyperfine splitting relative to intermediate-state lifetime. Resource context supports that the Yb inner-shell 4f^13 5d6s^2 (J=2) transition is experimentally observed and weakly allowed with resolved hyperfine/Zeeman structure, while earlier theory identifies this J=2 manifold as metastable and relevant for precision-clock operation. The requested output is a quantitative gate-time versus target-infidelity summary over 10^-2, 10^-3, 10^-4, and 10^-5, prioritizing fastest gates at <=10^-3 infidelity.

Estimate achievable gate fidelity under 1 W/cm^2 available intensity.

Prioritize fastest gate regime meeting <=10^-3 infidelity.
original_problem.request_text:
Title: Inner shell excited clock state gate
Abstract: Estimate achievable X-gate speeds and fidelities for a Yb-171 nuclear qubit using Raman transfer via a long-lived inner-shell J=2 intermediate (with 1 W/cm^2 available), limited by hyperfine splitting versus intermediate lifetime, and report gate-time vs target-infidelity tradeoffs (10^-2-10^-5).
Full Description: I want to make a good X-gate for qubits encoded in the 3P0 state in Yb-171. This is a nuclear qubit with F=1/2, so exactly two states. We want to make X-gates via Raman transfer with some intermediate state. The fidelity of such an X-gate is limited by the ratio of the hyperfine splitting over the lifetime of the intermediate state. In Yb-171, there are inner-shell-excited clock states available, where one of the f-shell electrons is excited to a d-state. The J=2 state is accessible using a electric quadrupole transition. This is a weak transition, so hard to excite, but the payoff is that the lifetime of the inner-shell excited state is practically infinite. Say I have 1 W/cm^2 of laser power available at the appropriate wavelength, what fidelity and what gatespeeds can we accomplish? Ideally the gate should be as fast as possible, with, say, 10^-3 infidelity or better. Maybe make a table with gatespeeds and fidelity targets 10^-2, 10^-3, 10^-4 and 10^-5.
Problem Type: Open Question
Difficulty: Frontier
Domain: Quantum Computing
Originator: Marius-Constantin Dinu
Compute Budget: 1 APU, Apple Silicon, 128GB
Target Deadline: 2026-03-11
Tags: quantum computing
Categories: Quantum Computing
Expected Artifacts: Paper, Code
Related Links: https://exphy.hhu.de/fileadmin/redaktion/Fakultaeten/Mathematisch-Naturwissenschaftliche_Fakultaet/Physik/AG_Schiller/documents/Dzuba_et_al_Testing_physics_beyond_the_standard_model_through_additional_clock_transitions_in_neutral_ytterbium_2018.pdf, https://link.aps.org/accepted/10.1103/PhysRevLett.120.173001, https://link.aps.org/pdf/10.1103/PhysRevA.106.013712, https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.09765
Dataset Links: None

package.install_command: pip install omegaxiv && ox install yb171-raman-gate==0.1.0
